output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace test_design_eas_database {

  struct Student {
    std::string SNo;
    std::string SName;
    int Sex;
    std::string Birthday;
    int Major;
    float Scorses;
    std::string Remark;
    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<std::string> SNo;
  };

  struct Course {
    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> CID;
    std::string CName;
    int Term;
    int CTime;
    float Credit;
  };

  struct Score {
    std::string SNo;
    int CID;
    float Score;
    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<std::string> SNo;
    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> CID;
    sqlgen::ForeignKey<std::string, Student, "SNo"> fk_student;
    sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Course, "CID"> fk_course;
  };

  void design_eas_database() {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn) {
      return;
    }

    conn->execute(
      "CREATE TABLE Student ("
      "SNo varchar(10) NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, "
      "SName varchar(30) NOT NULL, "
      "Sex int NULL DEFAULT 1, "
      "Birthday datetime NULL, "
      "Major int NULL, "
      "Scorses float NULL DEFAULT 0, "
      "Remark text NULL"
      ")"
    );

    conn->execute(
      "CREATE TABLE Course ("
      "CID int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, "
      "CName varchar(30) NOT NULL, "
      "Term int NULL DEFAULT 1, "
      "CTime int NULL, "
      "Credit float NOT NULL DEFAULT 0"
      ")"
    );

    conn->execute(
      "CREATE TABLE Score ("
      "SNo varchar(10) NOT NULL, "
      "CID int NOT NULL, "
      "Score float NULL, "
      "PRIMARY KEY (SNo, CID), "
      "FOREIGN KEY (SNo) REFERENCES Student(SNo), "
      "FOREIGN KEY (CID) REFERENCES Course(CID)"
      ")"
    );
  }

}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/aggregations.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/literals.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_product_sales_report {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string category;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct CategoryReport {
  std::string category;
  int total_qty;
  double total_amt;
};

struct ProductSalesReport {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ProductSalesReport";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string category;
  int total_qty;
  double total_amt;
};

std::vector<CategoryReport> query_product_sales_report() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Define column references with table aliases
  using p_category = sqlgen::Col<"category", "p">;
  using p_product_id = sqlgen::Col<"product_id", "p">;
  using s_product_id = sqlgen::Col<"product_id", "s">;
  using s_quantity = sqlgen::Col<"quantity", "s">;
  using s_amount = sqlgen::Col<"amount", "s">;
  
  // Build the query using sqlgen's fluent interface
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product, "p">(p_category{}, 
                                                sqlgen::sum(s_quantity{}).as<"total_qty">(),
                                                sqlgen::sum(s_amount{}).as<"total_amt">())
             | sqlgen::inner_join<Sale, "s">(p_product_id{} == s_product_id{})
             | sqlgen::group_by(p_category{})
             | sqlgen::where(sqlgen::sum(s_amount{}) > 5000)
             | sqlgen::to<std::vector<CategoryReport>>();
  
  // Connect to the database and execute the query
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return {}; // Return empty vector on connection error
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();
  
  return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace query_product_sales_report
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> book_id;
  std::string title;
  std::string author;
  int year;
  std::string genre;
  double price;
};

struct BookFilters {
  std::optional<std::string> title_keyword;
  std::optional<int> min_year;
  std::optional<std::string> genre;
  std::optional<double> max_price;
};

/**
 * Function: Search for books based on multiple optional criteria.
 * 
 * @param filters Optional filters for title, year, genre, and price
 * @return List of books matching the criteria
 */
std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& filters) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return {};
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;
  
  // Build the base query
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>("book_id"_c, "title"_c, "author"_c, "year"_c, "genre"_c, "price"_c);
  
  // Build the condition dynamically
  auto condition = 1 == 1; // Start with a true condition
  
  if (filters.title_keyword) {
    condition = condition && ("title"_c.like("%" + *filters.title_keyword + "%"));
  }
  if (filters.min_year) {
    condition = condition && ("year"_c >= *filters.min_year);
  }
  if (filters.genre) {
    condition = condition && ("genre"_c == *filters.genre);
  }
  if (filters.max_price) {
    condition = condition && ("price"_c <= *filters.max_price);
  }
  
  // Add the where clause to the query
  query = query | sqlgen::where(condition);
  
  // Execute the query and return results
  auto result = query(conn);
  if (result) {
    return *result;
  } else {
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace query_search_books_by_criteria
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace schema_create_complex_table {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> user_id;
  sqlgen::Unique<std::string> username;
  sqlgen::Unique<std::string> email;
  int age;
  std::string bio;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
  bool is_verified;
};

/**
 * Function: Create a table with various constraints and data types.
 * 
 * @return true if creation succeeds, false otherwise
 */
bool create_complex_table() {
  // Connect to the SQLite database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return false;
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;

  // Create the table using sqlgen::create_table
  auto create_result = sqlgen::create_table<UserProfile>(conn);
  return static_cast<bool>(create_result);
}

}  // namespace schema_create_complex_table
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_migration_add_column {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct UserAfter {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::string phone_number;
  bool is_active;
};

/**
 * Function: Perform schema migration to add new columns.
 * 
 * @return true if migration succeeds, false otherwise
 */
bool migration_add_column() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return false;
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;

  // Generate the current table structure for User
  auto create_table_stmt = sqlgen::transpilation::to_create_table<User>();
  
  // Convert the table structure to SQL (as per specification)
  std::string create_table_sql = sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(create_table_stmt);

  // Execute ALTER TABLE statements to add new columns
  auto result1 = conn->execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN phone_number VARCHAR(20);");
  if (!result1) {
    return false;
  }

  auto result2 = conn->execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1;");
  if (!result2) {
    return false;
  }

  return true;
}

} // namespace schema_migration_add_column
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
// eas_common.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

using namespace sqlgen::literals;

struct Student {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<sqlgen::Varchar<10>> SNo;
  sqlgen::Varchar<30> SName;
  int Sex = 1;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> Birthday;
  std::optional<uint32_t> Major;
  std::optional<double> Scorses = 0.0;
  std::optional<std::string> Remark;
  std::optional<int> Age;  // Added as per the problem
};

struct Course {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t, sqlgen::auto_incr> CID;
  sqlgen::Varchar<50> CName;
  std::optional<int> Term = 1;
  std::optional<int> CTime;
  int Credit = 0;
};

struct Score {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t, sqlgen::auto_incr> ScID;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<sqlgen::Varchar<10>, Student, "SNo"> SNo;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<uint32_t, Course, "CID"> CID;
  std::optional<double> Score;
  sqlgen::Varchar<1> Grade;  // Added as per the problem
};

struct Major {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t> MID;
  sqlgen::Varchar<30> MName;
};

const std::string db_path = "data/eas_database.db";

rfl::Result<Ref<sqlgen::sqlite::Connection>> design_eas_database_with_major() {
  std::filesystem::create_directories("data");
  return sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
      .and_then([](auto&& conn) {
        return sqlgen::exec(conn, "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;");
      })
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Student> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Course> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Score> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Major> | sqlgen::if_not_exists);
}

// update_eas_table.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

#include "eas_common.hpp"

namespace update_eas_table {

rfl::Result<Ref<sqlgen::sqlite::Connection>> update_eas_table() {
  return design_eas_database_with_major()
      .and_then(sqlgen::exec("ALTER TABLE Score ADD COLUMN Grade CHAR(1) NOT NULL"))
      .and_then(sqlgen::exec("ALTER TABLE Student ALTER COLUMN Age TYPE int"))
      .and_then(sqlgen::drop<Major>);
}

} // namespace update_eas_table

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct SalesSummary {
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string product_name;
  int total_quantity;
  double total_revenue;
};

/**
 * Function: Create a view SalesSummary that aggregates sales by product.
 * 
 * @return true if view creation succeeds, false otherwise
 */
bool create_view_sales_summary() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Build the SELECT query with JOIN and GROUP BY
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Sale, "s">(
      "p.name"_c | sqlgen::as<"product_name">,
      sqlgen::sum("s.quantity"_c) | sqlgen::as<"total_quantity">,
      sqlgen::sum("s.amount"_c) | sqlgen::as<"total_revenue">
  ) | sqlgen::join<Product, "p">("s.product_id"_c == "p.product_id"_c)
    | sqlgen::group_by("p.name"_c);

  // Create the view creation statement
  auto create_view_stmt = sqlgen::create_as<SalesSummary>(query);

  // Connect to the SQLite database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result.is_ok()) {
    return false;
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  // Convert the statement to SQL and execute
  std::string sql = sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(create_view_stmt);
  auto execute_result = conn->execute(sql);

  // Return execution status
  return execute_result.is_ok();
}

}  // namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_temporal_data {

struct Event {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
  std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct TemporalAnalysis {
  int event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  int start_day;
  int start_month;
  int start_year;
  int start_hour;
  int start_minute;
  int start_second;
  int start_weekday;
  int64_t start_epoch;
  std::optional<double> duration_days;
};

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data(const std::vector<Event>& events) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to SQLite database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();
  
  // Create table and insert events using sqlgen's write functionality
  auto write_result = sqlgen::write(conn, events).value();
  
  // Define column references
  auto event_id_c = "event_id"_c;
  auto event_name_c = "event_name"_c;
  auto start_time_c = "start_time"_c;
  auto end_time_c = "end_time"_c;
  
  // Construct complex query with multiple operations
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Event>(
      event_id_c,
      event_name_c,
      sqlgen::day(start_time_c).as<"start_day">(),
      sqlgen::month(start_time_c).as<"start_month">(),
      sqlgen::year(start_time_c).as<"start_year">(),
      sqlgen::hour(start_time_c).as<"start_hour">(),
      sqlgen::minute(start_time_c).as<"start_minute">(),
      sqlgen::second(start_time_c).as<"start_second">(),
      sqlgen::weekday(start_time_c).as<"start_weekday">(),
      sqlgen::unixepoch(start_time_c).as<"start_epoch">(),
      sqlgen::days_between(start_time_c, end_time_c).as<"duration_days">()
  ) | sqlgen::where(end_time_c.is_not_null()) | sqlgen::to<std::vector<TemporalAnalysis>>();
  
  // Execute query and return results
  return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace analyze_temporal_data
```</answer>

bool upsert_inventory_items(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  // Connect to the SQLite database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace analyze_social_network {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::string email;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> join_date;
  std::string city;
};

struct Friendship {
  int user1_id;
  int user2_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct FriendConnection {
  std::string user1_name;
  std::string user2_name;
};

std::vector<FriendConnection> analyze_social_network_impl(const std::vector<User>& users, const std::vector<Friendship>& friendships) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to in-memory database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect();
  
  // Create tables and insert data
  sqlgen::write(conn, users);
  sqlgen::write(conn, friendships);
  
  // Build and execute the query for mutual friends
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<User, "u1">("username"_t1.as<"user1_name">(), "username"_t2.as<"user2_name">())
               | sqlgen::inner_join<Friendship, "f1">("u1"_t1."user_id" == "f1"_t1."user_id1")
               | sqlgen::inner_join<Friendship, "f2">("f1"_t1."user_id2" == "f2"_t1."user_id1")
               | sqlgen::inner_join<User, "u2">("f2"_t1."user_id2" == "u2"_t1."user_id")
               | sqlgen::where("u1"_t1."user_id" < "u2"_t1."user_id")
               | sqlgen::group_by("u1"_t1."user_id", "u2"_t1."user_id")
               | sqlgen::having(sqlgen::count("f1"_t1."user_id2") > 0)
               | sqlgen::to<std::vector<FriendConnection>>();
  
  return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace analyze_social_network
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
};

struct MathAnalysis {
  int product_id;
  std::string name;
  double abs_price;
  double ceil_price;
  double floor_price;
  double rounded_price;
  double exp_discount;
  double ln_price;
  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations(const std::vector<Product>& products) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to SQLite database
  auto connection = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();
  
  // Create Product table
  const std::string create_table_sql = R"(
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Product (
      product_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
      price REAL NOT NULL,
      quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
      discount_rate REAL NOT NULL
    )
  )";
  connection.execute(create_table_sql).value();
  
  // Insert products
  sqlgen::write(connection, products).value();
  
  // Build SELECT query with mathematical operations
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>(
    "product_id"_c,
    "name"_c,
    sqlgen::abs("price"_c).as<"abs_price">(),
    sqlgen::ceil("price"_c).as<"ceil_price">(),
    sqlgen::floor("price"_c).as<"floor_price">(),
    sqlgen::round("price"_c, 2).as<"rounded_price">(),
    sqlgen::exp("discount_rate"_c / 100).as<"exp_discount">(),
    sqlgen::ln("price"_c).as<"ln_price">(),
    sqlgen::log2("quantity"_c).as<"log2_quantity">(),
    sqlgen::sin("discount_rate"_c).as<"sin_discount">(),
    sqlgen::cos("discount_rate"_c).as<"cos_discount">(),
    sqlgen::tan("discount_rate"_c).as<"tan_discount">(),
    sqlgen::sqrt("quantity"_c).as<"sqrt_quantity">()
  ) | sqlgen::to<std::vector<MathAnalysis>>;
  
  // Execute query and return results
  return query(connection).value();
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>
#include <cctype>

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

/**
 * description: trim_and_length_validation.
 * This function demonstrates how to trim whitespace from string columns and validate their lengths using sqlgen read API.
 * It returns a vector of ValidatedProfile structs, where each struct contains the user_id, trimmed_name, trimmed_email, name_length, and email_length.
 * Returns: std::vector<ValidatedProfile>.
 */
std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  // Connect to the SQLite database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return {};
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;

  // Read all UserProfile records
  auto users_result = sqlgen::read<std::vector<UserProfile>>(conn);
  if (!users_result) {
    return {};
  }
  auto users = *users_result;

  std::vector<ValidatedProfile> result;
  for (const auto& user : users) {
    ValidatedProfile vp;
    vp.user_id = user.user_id;

    // Trim the name using C++ string algorithms
    std::string trimmed_name = user.name;
    // Remove leading spaces
    trimmed_name.erase(trimmed_name.begin(), std::find_if(trimmed_name.begin(), trimmed_name.end(), [](unsigned char c) { return !std::isspace(c); }));
    // Remove trailing spaces
    trimmed_name.erase(std::find_if(trimmed_name.rbegin(), trimmed_name.rend(), [](unsigned char c) { return !std::isspace(c); }).base(), trimmed_name.end());
    vp.trimmed_name = trimmed_name;

    // Trim the email using C++ string algorithms
    std::string trimmed_email = user.email;
    trimmed_email.erase(trimmed_email.begin(), std::find_if(trimmed_email.begin(), trimmed_email.end(), [](unsigned char c) { return !std::isspace(c); }));
    trimmed_email.erase(std::find_if(trimmed_email.rbegin(), trimmed_email.rend(), [](unsigned char c) { return !std::isspace(c); }).base(), trimmed_email.end());
    vp.trimmed_email = trimmed_email;

    // Compute lengths (could also be done in SQL, but here we use C++ for demonstration)
    vp.name_length = user.name.length();
    vp.email_length = user.email.length();

    result.push_back(vp);
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```

**Note:** The solution uses C++ string manipulation for trimming since the reference materials do not provide direct SQL trim functions in the `transpilation` namespace. The lengths are computed in C++ as well. If SQL-based trimming is required, additional `transpilation` functions would be needed, which are not included in the provided reference materials.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace calculate_order_metrics {

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  std::optional<double> discount_rate;
  std::optional<double> tax_rate;
};

struct OrderMetrics {
  int item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
  double tax_rate;
  double gross_total;
  double discounted_total;
  double final_total;
  double unit_cost;
  int quantity_group;
  double estimated_profit;
};

std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(const std::vector<OrderItem>& items) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to SQLite database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return {};
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();
  
  // Write OrderItem data to database
  auto write_result = sqlgen::write(conn, items);
  if (!write_result) {
    return {};
  }
  
  // Build complex query with arithmetic operations
  auto query = select_from<OrderItem>(
    "item_id"_c,
    "order_id"_c, 
    "product_id"_c,
    "unit_price"_c,
    "quantity"_c,
    coalesce("discount_rate"_c, 0.0).as<"discount_rate">(),
    coalesce("tax_rate"_c, 0.0).as<"tax_rate">(),
    
    // Gross total calculation
    ("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c).as<"gross_total">(),
    
    // Discounted total with COALESCE for null handling
    (("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c) * (1 - coalesce("discount_rate"_c, 0.0)/100)).as<"discounted_total">(),
    
    // Final total with tax
    (("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c) * (1 - coalesce("discount_rate"_c, 0.0)/100) * 
     (1 + coalesce("tax_rate"_c, 0.0)/100)).as<"final_total">(),
    
    // Unit cost calculation
    ("unit_price"_c).as<"unit_cost">(),
    
    // Quantity grouping with modulo
    ("quantity"_c % 10).as<"quantity_group">(),
    
    // Estimated profit calculation
    ("unit_price"_c * 0.3).as<"estimated_profit">()
  );
  
  // Execute query and return results
  auto result = (read<std::vector<OrderMetrics>> | query)(conn);
  if (result) {
    return result.value();
  } else {
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace calculate_order_metrics
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

/**
 * description: replace_text_in_notes.
 * This function demonstrates how to replace text in notes using sqlgen update API.
 * It replaces all occurrences of "foo" with "bar" in the content column of the Note table.
 * Returns: std::vector<UpdatedNote>.
 */
std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();
  
  // Create update statement with replacement
  auto update_stmt = sqlgen::update<Note>(
    "content"_c.set(sqlgen::replace("content"_c, "foo", "bar"))
  );
  
  // Execute the update
  update_stmt(conn).value();
  
  // Read and convert updated notes
  auto updated_notes = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Note>>(conn).value();
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> result;
  
  for (const auto& note : updated_notes) {
    result.push_back(UpdatedNote{
      note.note_id,
      note.content  // After replacement, content now contains "bar"
    });
  }
  
  return result;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/literals.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
  std::string location;
};

bool inventory_bulk_write_and_read(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to SQLite database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result.is_ok()) {
    return false;
  }
  auto connection = conn_result.value();
  
  // Drop existing table if exists
  auto drop_result = (sqlgen::drop<Inventory> | sqlgen::if_exists)(connection);
  if (!drop_result.is_ok()) {
    return false;
  }
  
  // Create new table
  const std::string create_sql = R"(
    CREATE TABLE Inventory (
      item_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
      location TEXT
    )
  )";
  auto create_result = connection.execute(create_sql);
  if (!create_result.is_ok()) {
    return false;
  }
  
  // Bulk write items
  auto write_obj = sqlgen::write(items);
  auto write_result = connection.write(write_obj.data_.begin(), write_obj.data_.end());
  if (!write_result.is_ok()) {
    return false;
  }
  
  // Read filtered and ordered results
  auto read_result = (sqlgen::read<std::vector<Inventory>> 
                      | sqlgen::where("quantity"_c > 50) 
                      | sqlgen::order_by("quantity"_c.desc()))(connection);
  
  if (read_result.is_ok()) {
    return !read_result.value().empty();
  }
  
  return false;
}

}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace concat_function {

struct Person {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
};

struct FullName {
  std::string full_name;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates CONCAT usage.
 * Returns: query value.
 */
inline auto concat_function() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;
    
    return sqlgen::select_from<Person>(
        sqlgen::concat("first_name"_c, " ", "last_name"_c) | sqlgen::as<"full_name">
    ) | sqlgen::to<FullName>;
}

}  // namespace concat_function
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace get_top_products {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double price;
};

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Date sale_date;
};

struct TopProduct {
  int product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double total_sales;
};

std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count = 10) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();
  
  // Build the query using sqlgen's fluent interface
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product, "p">(
                  "product_id"_c, 
                  "product_name"_c, 
                  "category"_c,
                  ("price"_c * "quantity"_c).as<"total_sales">)
              | sqlgen::inner_join<Sales, "s">("product_id"_c == "product_id"_c)
              | sqlgen::group_by("product_id"_c, "product_name"_c, "category"_c)
              | sqlgen::order_by(sqlgen::desc("total_sales"_c))
              | sqlgen::limit(limit_count);
  
  // Execute the query and convert results to vector
  auto range = query(conn);
  return sqlgen::internal::collect::vector(range);
}

}  // namespace get_top_products
```

**Explanation of key components:**

1. **Database Connection**: Uses `sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite")` to establish connection
2. **Query Construction**: 
   - Starts with `select_from<Product, "p">` to select from Product table with alias "p"
   - Selects required fields and calculates total sales using `("price"_c * "quantity"_c).as<"total_sales">`
   - Joins with Sales table using `inner_join<Sales, "s">` with join condition
   - Groups by product fields using `group_by`
   - Orders by total sales in descending order using `order_by(sqlgen::desc("total_sales"_c))`
   - Applies limit using `limit(limit_count)`
3. **Execution**: The query is executed by calling it with the connection `query(conn)`
4. **Result Conversion**: Uses `sqlgen::internal::collect::vector` to convert the range to `std::vector<TopProduct>`

This implementation follows sqlgen's principles of using a fluent interface with operator overloading (`|`) to build queries in a readable manner.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace create_view_order_summary {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
};

struct OrderSummary {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "OrderSummaryView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
  double total;
};

/**
 * Description: This function demonstrates creating a view.
 * Returns: std::vector<OrderSummary>.
 */
std::vector<OrderSummary> create_view_order_summary() {
    // Connect to the database
    auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn_result) {
        throw std::runtime_error(conn_result.error().what());
    }
    auto conn = conn_result.value();

    // Create the view
    std::string create_view_sql = R"(
        CREATE VIEW OrderSummaryView AS
        SELECT o.order_id, o.customer, SUM(oi.unit_price*oi.quantity) as total
        FROM Order o INNER JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id=oi.order_id
        GROUP BY o.order_id, o.customer;
    )";

    auto execute_result = conn->execute(create_view_sql);
    if (!execute_result) {
        throw std::runtime_error(execute_result.error().what());
    }

    // Read the view
    auto read_result = sqlgen::read<std::vector<OrderSummary>>(*conn);
    if (!read_result) {
        throw std::runtime_error(read_result.error().what());
    }

    return read_result.value();
}

}  // namespace create_view_order_summary
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace left_join_explicit {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

inline std::string like_pattern_matching() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto query = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Product>> | sqlgen::where("name"_c.like("A%"));
  return sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(query);
}

}  // namespace left_join_explicit
```</answer>
